title: Dryopteris welwitschii
taxonomy: Dryopteris welwitschii belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae and the order Blechnales. It is classified under the genus Dryopteris, which includes species of wood ferns. The species is further classified into the subgenus Dryopteris and section Phaeospora. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae. It is also classified under the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes ferns and their allies.
uses: Dryopteris welwitschii has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. It is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal value. The plant is used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. In addition, it is also used to treat skin problems and wounds due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Dryopteris welwitschii for medicinal purposes.

constituents: Dryopteris welwitschii is a species of fern known for its potential medicinal properties. Its active constituents are primarily saponins and flavonoids, which are responsible for its various health benefits. parts: Dryopteris welwitschii is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Africa.
